title: Restrict Network ACL Rules
console_steps: 2. Click 'Network ACLs' in the left nav
3. Select the NACL with overly permissive rules
4. Click 'Inbound rules' tab, then 'Edit inbound rules'
5. Remove or restrict rules allowing 0.0.0.0/0 on all ports
6. Add specific allow rules for required traffic only
7. Ensure deny rules are properly ordered
cli_command: aws ec2 replace-network-acl-entry --network-acl-id {NACL_ID} --rule-number {RULE_NUM} --protocol tcp --port-range From={FROM_PORT},To={TO_PORT} --cidr-block {CIDR} --rule-action allow --ingress
cli_example: aws ec2 replace-network-acl-entry --network-acl-id acl-0123456789abcdef0 --rule-number 100 --protocol tcp --port-range From=443,To=443 --cidr-block 10.0.0.0/8 --rule-action allow --ingress
terraform_snippet:
resource "aws_network_acl_rule" "https_in" {
  network_acl_id = aws_network_acl.main.id
  rule_number    = 100
  egress         = false
  protocol       = "tcp"
  rule_action    = "allow"
  cidr_block     = "10.0.0.0/8"
  from_port      = 443
  to_port        = 443
}

references.title: Ensure NACLs do not allow unrestricted inbound traffic

rollback_difficulty: Easy — NACL rules can be reverted
